title: Community Relations: Advertising and Fundraising
description: Conroe ISD's advertising and fundraising policy sets the rules for when and how outside organizations can promote products or services to students, and how fundraising activities are conducted in schools. It helps ensure that commercial and fundraising efforts on campus support the district's educational mission and do not disrupt learning. Parents and community members can refer to this policy to understand what kinds of promotions and fundraisers are permitted in district schools.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's GKB (Local) Community Relations: Advertising and Fundraising policy adds a new requirement that advertising be rejected if it is not aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local policies. No language was removed.
changes_added: Advertising shall be rejected if not aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local.
timeline_local.notes: The red underlined text on page 2 is clearly identifiable. No red strikethrough text was present in the document. The cover page title reads "Discuss and Possibly Adopt Revisions to Board Policy GKB (Local)" which maps to action = 'adopted'.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's GKB (Local) policy on Community Relations: Advertising and Fundraising adds a new sentence requiring that advertising be rejected if it is not aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with four referenced local policies (EMB, EFA, EFB, and EMI Local).
changes_added: Advertising shall be rejected if not aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local.
timeline_local.notes: The added text appears in red underlined font on page 2 of the document, immediately following the existing list of five advertising rejection criteria. No red strikethrough (removed) text was observed in the redline. The cover page title heading reads "Discuss Revisions to Board Policy GKB (Local)," indicating a first reading.

description_local: This policy governs what types of advertising and fundraising activities are allowed on school district property and through district communications. It applies to outside organizations, businesses, and community groups that want to promote products, services, or fundraising campaigns through the district. Parents and community members should be aware that the district now has clear guidelines requiring all advertising to align with district values and meet specific standards, giving the school board greater control over the messages students and families are exposed to through school channels.
